title: dryoathyrium edentulum
intro: Dryoathyrium edentulum, also known as the toothless oak fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is native to certain regions, particularly in mountainous areas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health issues. Dryoathyrium edentulum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to promote digestive health, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system.
